steps.title: Set up the integral
steps.body: Evaluate $\displaystyle\int_0^{\pi/2} \left(3\cos(x) + 2e^x\right) \mathrm{d}x$. First find the antiderivative term by term, using $\int \cos(x)\,\mathrm{d}x = \sin(x)$ and $\int e^x\,\mathrm{d}x = e^x$.

steps.title: Check by differentiating back
steps.body: $\dfrac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{d}x}\left(3\sin(x) + 2e^x\right) = 3\cos(x) + 2e^x$ — exactly the original integrand, confirming the antiderivative is correct.
